title: House GOP cancels WPR vote for 2nd straight day — feared it would pass due to absences, postponed to June after Memorial Day recess
snippet: House GOP leaders cancelled a vote Thursday on a resolution to limit President Trump's war powers in Iran, as Republicans were on the verge of losing the vote due to absences. The resolution was introduced by Rep. Gregory Meeks (D-NY), ranking member of House Foreign Affairs Committee. GOP leadership cancelled a similar vote Wednesday. The vote is now expected early June after Memorial Day recess. Three Republicans broke with party: Barrett, Fitzpatrick, Massie.
tag_reason: GOP cancellation of House WPR vote is a new development. Previous src-1237 reported upcoming 4th vote; this is the result — cancellation for 2nd straight day, postponement to June. New actor Gregory Meeks identified.

entities.name: Gregory Meeks
entities.role: House Foreign Affairs Committee Ranking Member
